quality_expectations: must begin from GEOFlow's existing frontend contract and current theme system
must discover available themes and keep target_theme_id explicit before editing
must choose edit_theme, clone, or hybrid mode before proposing outputs

must detect current homepage data signals such as homepageCarouselSlides, homepageModules, homepageStyle, showHomepageModules, hotArticles, featuredArticles, articles, cardSummaries, and public theme assets when homepage enrichment is requested
must detect HomepageModuleBuilder, supported module types, style fields, presets, and homepage import routes when the current GEOFlow workspace exposes them
must produce reviewed homepage-design.json with style, modules, and replace-or-append import guidance when homepage builder design is the safest path
must provide data source and fallback behavior for every added homepage module
must keep default homepage enrichment separate from search, category, and category-missing states unless explicitly requested
must preserve routing, SEO, and data-query boundaries unless explicitly expanded
must prefer preview-first theme forks or preview-safe packages over direct live replacement
must map reference styles or optimization goals onto GEOFlow modules instead of copying raw pages verbatim
must present finalize choices as publish_as_new_theme, replace_base_theme, or import_homepage_design_after_confirmation only after preview or payload review
